summary: Added the first freeze-point for the new PPT parallel architecture: a new `commit_design_spec` tool and a corresponding high-priority Chinese rule block in `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/SKILL.md` requiring explicit user confirmation before freezing the design contract. The tool persists confirmed design specs into `sys_ppt_drafts` fields (`design`, `outline`, `total_pages`), caches a design snapshot and per-page placeholder state in Redis (`ppt:design:*`, `ppt:page:*`, `ppt:progress:*`), writes `design_spec.json`/`design_spec.md` into the project folder when available, and emits a `design_spec_committed` event. This is the first step toward the main-context + page-worker model; no parallel worker orchestration was added yet.
implementation: Implemented a new `commit_design_spec` Go tool in `mcp-tool/ppt-master` with validation for confirmed user approval, page outline parsing, versioning, DB persistence, Redis snapshot caching, and project-file emission. Updated `ppt-master/SKILL.md` with a project-specific high-priority Chinese concurrency rule block that allows the self-hosted Go agent architecture while still requiring the old external-skill safeguards to be respected in spirit. The tool is registered via `init()` and the repo builds successfully.
risks: The new tool currently reuses existing draft fields instead of adding dedicated tables, so later work must keep the JSON schema stable. Redis page placeholder values are stored as generic JSON maps and the page-worker scheduler has not been implemented yet, so no actual parallel page generation occurs yet. The skill rule block is now bilingual-heavy and must stay aligned with the eventual prompt contract for DeepSeek.

summary: Added the first `regen_pages` tool skeleton for the new PPT page-level regeneration architecture. The tool requires `commit_design_spec` to have run, accepts `page_ids`, `mode` (`draft`/`fill`/`render`/`repair`), and an optional reason, creates a `ppt_task_*` task id, writes task state to Redis, updates per-page Redis contexts from `queued` to `running`, and emits `page_status` events for each stage. It does not call child agents yet. Updated `ppt-master/SKILL.md` to explain that `regen_pages` only creates page task status/SSE events in v1 and that real SVG updates continue to use `slide_content_ready` for same-page replacement. Added `regen_pages` to `skill_events.json` with `post: page_task_started`.
implementation: Implemented `mcp-tool/ppt-master/regen_pages.go` as a registered tool using existing Redis key helpers from `commit_design_spec.go`. It validates design commitment via session metadata, validates modes and page ids, stores `ppt:task:{taskId}`, updates `ppt:page:{draftId}:{pageIndex}` JSON state, and emits `page_status` events. No AI worker orchestration, draft SVG generation, or concurrency pool was added yet.
risks: `regen_pages` currently depends on `designSpecCommitted` session metadata being present after `commit_design_spec`; if Redis has design state but session metadata is missing, it will reject. Page state updates require Redis; if Redis is nil, task acceptance succeeds but no page context is persisted. This is intentional for the skeleton but should be tightened when real workers are added.

summary: Extended `regen_pages` from a status-only skeleton into a mock page draft worker. After accepting a page regen task and emitting `queued`/`running` page_status events, it now starts one goroutine per requested page, waits briefly, loads the page context from Redis, generates a gray skeleton placeholder SVG, writes it to `<ProjectPath>/svg_output/NN_title_draft.svg` when ProjectPath exists, updates Redis page stage to `draft_ready`, emits `page_status`, and emits `slide_content_ready` with full SVG content for same-page frontend replacement. This validates the frontend flow before adding real DeepSeek child-agent workers.
implementation: Added mock worker job struct and helper functions for Redis page context loading, safe draft SVG path creation, XML escaping, placeholder SVG generation, and same-page `slide_content_ready` emission. The worker runs asynchronously after `regen_pages` accepts a task. It does not call AI and does not perform validation beyond writing a placeholder SVG and state transition.
risks: The mock worker uses background context and does not yet support cancellation, concurrency limiting, retry policy, or task completion aggregation. If Redis is unavailable, it still emits a placeholder with fallback title but state persistence is skipped. The generated SVG is a generic skeleton, not template-derived yet.

summary: Added the first code-driven layout draft generator for PPT content pages. New `layout_draft.go` detects content safe areas from explicit comments like `CONTENT_SAFE_AREA`/`ĺŹŻç”¨ç©şé—´`, from `{{CONTENT_AREA}}` placeholder rectangles, or from a conservative fallback. It chooses among five common patterns (`content_3_cards`, `content_2_col`, `content_chart_right`, `content_table`, `content_timeline`) based on page context keywords, and produces a structured `layoutDraft` with regions, slots, max lines/chars, and font sizes. `regen_pages` mock worker now reads the page's template SVG when possible, builds/saves the layout draft into Redis page state, renders the draft as a placeholder SVG, writes it to `svg_output`, and emits `slide_content_ready` for frontend replacement.
implementation: Implemented safe area detection, pattern selection, layout region generation, layout-draft rendering, and Redis persistence of `layoutDraft`. Added tests for safe-area detection via comment and content-area rect, plus pattern selection. The generator is intentionally conservative and content-page focused; cover/toc/ending are not targeted yet.
tests: Ran `go test ./mcp-tool/ppt-master` successfully and `go build ./...` successfully.
risks: Template lookup currently tries project templates plus `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/<templateFile>`, which may not resolve layout-set subdirectories unless page outlines include a path relative to layouts. Safe-area parsing is heuristic and should later be strengthened with explicit `data-role="content-area"` or `CONTENT_SAFE_AREA` conventions in templates.

summary: Added a root-level `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md` documenting future frontend template-authoring conventions for the code-driven PPT layout draft pipeline. It defines explicit content safe area comments, `data-role="content-area"`, standard placeholders, current layout patterns, frontend template tool requirements, fallback behavior, and product boundaries. Verified `go test ./mcp-tool/ppt-master` and `go build ./...` still pass. Noted an unrelated existing modification in `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/china_unicom_uniai/02_toc.svg` that was not touched.
implementation: Created a Markdown authoring guide for content-page template contracts: `CONTENT_SAFE_AREA`, `{{CONTENT_AREA}}`, coordinate rules, placeholders, layoutDraft output shape, frontend authoring UI requirements, backend fallback, and reliability/product boundaries.
risks: The guide is documentation only; existing templates are not yet migrated to include `CONTENT_SAFE_AREA` or `data-role="content-area"`. The backend parser currently supports comments and `{{CONTENT_AREA}}` but not `data-role` as a first-class path yet.

summary: Hardened the content safe-area detection and template lookup for the code-driven PPT layout draft pipeline. `layout_draft.go` now treats `<rect data-role="content-area" ...>` as the highest-priority safe-area source, ahead of comments and `{{CONTENT_AREA}}` heuristics. `regen_pages.go` template lookup now supports both full layout-relative template paths and base filenames as fallback. Added a unit test proving `data-role="content-area"` overrides the comment fallback. This aligns backend parsing with the new `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md` contract for the future frontend template authoring tool.
implementation: Added `detectDataRoleContentArea` and `hasAttrValue`, called first in `detectContentSafeArea`. Extended template SVG candidate lookup in `readTemplateSVG` to include both `layouts/<templateFile>` and `layouts/<base(templateFile)>`. Added `TestDetectContentSafeAreaFromDataRole`.
risks: The base-filename template fallback can only find root-level layout files, not files nested under a selected layout pack if `templateFile` only contains `03_content.svg`; future page outlines should prefer full paths like `china_unicom_uniai/03_content.svg` or project templates should be copied into project/templates.

summary: Added template-pack-level layout preferences for code-driven PPT content layout drafts. `layout_draft.go` now reads optional `layout_config.json` from project templates or resource layout pack directories. The config supports `defaultContentPattern` and `allowedPatterns`; strong page content signals still win when allowed, otherwise the engine falls back to the template default. `regen_pages` now passes the config into layout draft generation. Added a sample `layout_config.json` for `china_unicom_uniai` and documented this contract in `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md`. Tests cover template default behavior and allowed-pattern fallback.
implementation: Introduced `layoutConfig`, `readLayoutConfig`, and candidate path resolution. Pattern selection now accepts a config, applies known-pattern validation, respects `allowedPatterns`, and uses `defaultContentPattern` as fallback. Added guide docs for optional template-level layout preferences.
risks: Only `china_unicom_uniai` currently has a sample config. Project template lookup may prefer project-level `templates/layout_config.json` over layout-pack config; this is intentional but should be understood by the future frontend template authoring tool.

summary: Added the first page-agent input package schema for the future DeepSeek child-agent workers. New `page_agent_context.go` builds a clean per-page JSON package containing `designSpec`, `page` context, `layoutDraft`, template info, task/mode/page identifiers, design version, and instructions that forbid coordinate changes. `regen_pages` mock worker now builds this context after layout draft generation, stores it in Redis at `ppt:pagectx:{draftId}:{pageIndex}`, and writes it to `<ProjectPath>/page_contexts/NN_context.json` for inspection. Added tests for the page context key and safe-area source labeling.
implementation: Implemented `pageAgentContext`, `templateInfo`, `buildPageAgentContext`, Redis cache/write-to-file helpers, safe area source labeling, and integration in `runMockPageDraft`. The context package is deliberately structured and clean so future DeepSeek child workers do not need full main chat history.
risks: The generated page-agent context currently depends on Redis for committed design lookup; if Redis misses, `designSpec` is nil. A later version may need DB fallback from `sys_ppt_drafts.design`. Instructions are still static strings and should be tuned when the real child-agent prompt is implemented.

summary: Finished the DB fallback hardening for PPT page-agent context preparation and confirmed the existing agent engine reuse boundary. `loadCommittedDesign` already falls back from Redis to `sys_ppt_drafts.design/outline` and repopulates Redis before child context construction. This pass added page-context fallback in `regen_pages`: if `ppt:page:{draftId}:{pageIndex}` is missing or invalid, it rebuilds the page context from `sys_ppt_drafts.outline/design`, then writes the reconstructed page context back into Redis. This keeps child workers on clean per-page JSON packages and avoids DB access inside the child agent execution phase.
implementation: Updated `mcp-tool/ppt-master/regen_pages.go` to import `model/system` and `gorm.io/datatypes`, add `loadPageContextFromDB`, `cacheLoadedPageContext`, and `designVersionFromDraft`, and route Redis misses/invalid JSON through the DB fallback. Added a focused unit test for design version parsing in `page_agent_context_test.go`. The intended fallback timing is now explicit: parent/mock worker constructs the pageAgentContext, restores hot state from DB if needed, then future child agents can reuse `agent_engine.Run` with an isolated session/context package without querying DB during their own reasoning loop.
tests: Ran `gofmt -w mcp-tool/ppt-master/page_agent_context.go mcp-tool/ppt-master/regen_pages.go mcp-tool/ppt-master/page_agent_context_test.go`, `go test ./mcp-tool/ppt-master`, and `go build ./...` successfully.
risks: The real DeepSeek child-agent runner is not wired yet; this only hardens the context package and fallback path. Current page fallback reconstructs from outline/design and uses a generic page title if the requested page is absent from outline. Existing unrelated modification in `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/china_unicom_uniai/02_toc.svg` was not touched.

summary: Started the online PPT template authoring backend by adding a global template resource library API. Added authenticated PPT resource routes for uploading, listing, and soft-deleting template resources, plus a new static prefix for global resources.
implementation: Added `POST /aiPpt/templateResources/upload`, `GET /aiPpt/templateResources`, and `DELETE /aiPpt/templateResources`. Upload accepts one `file` plus optional `name/category/tags`; `.svg` files are classified as `icon` and saved under `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/icons/custom`, while `.png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp` files are classified as `image` and saved under `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/images/custom`. Metadata is persisted in `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/resource_index.json`; delete is soft-delete in that index so existing template references do not break. Added `/ppt-template-resources` static mapping to serve `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates`, and added SPA API-prefix exclusion. SVG upload uses MVP security rejection for `<script>`, `foreignObject`, event handlers, `javascript:`, and unsafe data URLs. Added permission/API initialization entries for the three new endpoints.
tests: Ran `go test ./api/v1/system ./service/system ./router/system ./model/system/request ./initialize ./source/system ./cmd/init_ppt_api -run "^$" -vet=off` successfully and `go build ./...` successfully.
risks: The resource index is JSON-file based with no locking; concurrent uploads could race. SVG sanitization is conservative but not a full XML sanitizer. Existing `.env` modification was pre-existing/unrelated and not touched.

summary: Added a draft template project layer for PPT template authoring. This gives an intermediate workspace between uploaded source images/resources and the published `layouts/{template_id}` output.
implementation: Added project CRUD and page-saving endpoints under `/aiPpt/templateProjects` plus a `project.json`-based local draft store at `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/template_projects/{projectId}/`. The project layer stores summary metadata in `projects_index.json`, page SVGs in `pages/*.svg`, and supports create/list/detail/update/save-page flows. Added request models for project create/update/search/page-save. Integrated the project routes into `router/system/sys_ppt.go` and registered API/casbin/init entries. The existing global resource library remains separate and reusable by projects. The resource/static routing and API prefix whitelist already include `/ppt-template-resources` from the prior step.
tests: Ran `go test ./api/v1/system ./service/system ./router/system ./model/system/request ./initialize ./source/system ./cmd/init_ppt_api -run "^$" -vet=off` successfully and `go build ./...` successfully.
risks: Project persistence is file-based JSON and currently has no locking, so concurrent writes could race. The page-save path stores raw SVG after lightweight sanitization; it is not a full XML security pipeline yet. This is sufficient for the draft workflow but should be hardened before multi-user concurrent editing.

summary: Added Gemini-powered image analysis for PPT template draft projects.
implementation: Added `POST /aiPpt/templateProjects/analyze?id=...`. The service reads the draft project's `sourceImageUrl`, resolves `/ppt-template-resources/...` to the local resource file, sends the image plus a strict JSON prompt through the existing `agent_engine.GeminiProvider` using `[]genai.Part` and `genai.ImageData`, parses Gemini's JSON response into the project's `analysis`, generates a `template_brief.md`, stores the markdown in the project `brief`, and writes `template_brief.md` beside `project.json`. The prompt asks Gemini to classify elements as SVG primitives, image assets, or needs-review; identify content safe area; list asset placeholders and placeholder suggestions; and warn about missing logos/watermarks/textures. Added route/API handler and init/casbin entries.
tests: Ran `go test ./api/v1/system ./service/system ./router/system ./model/system/request ./source/system ./cmd/init_ppt_api -run "^$" -vet=off` successfully and `go build ./...` successfully.
risks: Gemini output is prompt-enforced JSON, not schema-enforced via response_mime_type/response_schema, so parsing may fail on non-JSON responses. Only local `/ppt-template-resources/...` source images are supported initially. The analysis endpoint makes a real Gemini API call and was compile-tested but not live API tested in this session.

summary: Continued PPT template authoring backend by adding validation and publish endpoints after the resource upload/project/analyze/generateDraft flow.
implementation: Added `GET /aiPpt/templateProjects/validate?id=...` and `POST /aiPpt/templateProjects/publish?id=...`. Validation checks project/page presence, SVG safety, recommended `viewBox="0 0 1280 720"`, required `CONTENT_SAFE_AREA` plus `data-role="content-area"`, and local `/ppt-template-resources/...` href/xlink:href existence. Publish first runs validation, then writes pages to `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/{templateId}/`, writes `design_spec.md` from the project brief, and upserts the layout entry in `layouts_index.json`. Added API handlers, routes, and API/casbin/init-script permission entries. Resource upload already returns `resourcePath` equal to the static URL so frontend can use it directly.
risks: File-based JSON indexes still have no write locking, so concurrent publish/upload could race. Publish currently overwrites an existing `layouts/{templateId}` directory's matching page files and index entry, so UI should treat templateId as the publish key and warn on collisions if needed. `.env` is dirty but pre-existing/unrelated and was not touched.

summary: Added an API guide for AI/front-end use of the online PPT template authoring endpoints.
implementation: Created `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_API_GUIDE.md`. It documents the three-layer model (global resources, template project drafts, published templates), Redis session keys and TTL, full recommended workflow from source image upload through create/analyze/generateDraft/recentAssets/save/validate/publish, resource upload/list/delete APIs, project list/update APIs, published template APIs, response shapes, and AI guidance about SVG primitives vs image assets, avoiding base64, preserving content safe-area markers, and using placeholders.
tests: Checked the document with `rg` for key endpoint names and markers. No code tests were needed for this documentation-only change.
risks: Documentation assumes the current endpoint behavior from the latest backend changes; if the API shape changes, this guide should be updated with it.

summary: Added separate SVG and bitmap resource list endpoints and backend normalization that inlines SVG resources referenced through <image> tags when saving template pages.
implementation: Added `GET /aiPpt/templateResources/svg` and `GET /aiPpt/templateResources/bitmaps`, implemented as wrappers over the existing resource index filtered to `kind=icon` and `kind=image`. Added API handlers, routes, init API entries, and casbin entries. Updated `SaveTemplateProjectPage` to run `normalizeTemplateSVGResourceRefs` before saving: it detects `<image href="...svg">` or `xlink:href`, resolves only local `/ppt-template-resources/...` SVG assets, sanitizes the SVG asset, extracts its inner content/viewBox, and replaces the image tag with an inline `<svg data-role="inline-icon" ...>...</svg>` preserving x/y/width/height/preserveAspectRatio. Bitmap image hrefs remain unchanged. Updated `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_API_GUIDE.md` to document the two list endpoints and frontend/backend SVG inlining contract.
risks: SVG inlining is regex-based and handles normal single-tag `<image .../>` or `<image ...>` patterns with href/xlink:href ending in `.svg`; very unusual multiline or nested image syntax could need hardening. Inline transform preserves x/y/size but does not parse complex SVG transform attributes from the original image tag.

summary: Localized template category labels and layout summary descriptions in the PPT layouts index.
implementation: Updated `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/layouts_index.json` so category labels (`brand`, `general`, `scenario`, `government`, `special`) are Chinese and all layout `summary` fields are Chinese. Existing Chinese `label` and `desc` fields were preserved.
tests: Reviewed the diff for `label` and `summary` changes. No code tests were needed for this metadata-only JSON change.
risks: Only visible metadata changed; `tone`, `themeMode`, and `keywords` remain English because they appear to serve AI/template matching metadata rather than card display copy.

summary: Localized all PPT layout `tone` fields to Chinese.
implementation: Updated `resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/layouts_index.json`, replacing the English tone descriptions for all templates with concise Chinese tone descriptions. The previously updated Chongqing University tone was preserved.
tests: Ran `rg -n '"tone":' resource/ai_skills/ppt-master/templates/layouts/layouts_index.json` to verify all tone values are now Chinese.
risks: Metadata-only change; no runtime logic affected.
